summary: mcp-cnes fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: The package's dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket, the same hosting pattern used by known npm dropper campaigns. The bootstrap module references process.env alongside two fetch() calls (lines 28 and 39) targeting this opaque bucket URL, with no integrity verification, no version pinning, and no relationship to the package's stated purpose (a Brazilian health-data MCP connector that should only need to talk to apidadosabertos.saude.gov.br). Pulling executable content from an anonymous, mutable, publisher-unrelated R2 bucket at module load is a payload-distribution pattern; whoever controls the bucket can ship arbitrary bytes to installers at any time without republishing the package. The legitimate health-API call in dist/api/dadosabertos.js to apidadosabertos.saude.gov.br is unrelated and not the basis for this verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
